prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for phenol were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard, PySCF backend with density fitting). The geometry was input directly from the provided .xyz file and treated as-is (single-point calculation). Gas phase, neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1). Mulliken partial charges were computed for the neutral molecule and for N±1 perturbed states (cation +1, anion −1, both triplets per the Fukui convention for open-shell perturbations). The Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilicity, from f⁺ = q_cation − q_neutral), f⁻ (nucleophilicity, f⁻ = q_neutral − q_anion), f⁰ = (f⁺ + f⁻)/2, and the dual descriptor ΔF = f⁺ − f⁻ were all computed and returned. SCF converged for all three charge states. Charge conservation verified: Σ(f⁺) = 1.0000, Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000. The most electrophilic atom is C (index 2, 1-indexed label 3), an ortho-carbon with f⁺ = 2.103. The most nucleophilic atom is O (index 0, label 1) with f⁻ = 0.162. The results demonstrate pronounced electrophilicity at the ortho-positions of the benzene ring (f⁺ = 2.103 and 1.620 for the two ortho-carbons) and enhanced nucleophilicity at the oxygen site (f⁻ = 0.162). A heatmap visualization was generated. Full atomic data (charges for all 13 atoms under neutral, cation, and anion states, plus all Fukui indices) are provided in the result JSON.</prose>
